div1.entryFree: Jig, subst. a facetious metrical composition, a ludicrous ballad; or a merry dance accompanying it: all my merry --s are quite forgot, Pilgr. 253. a Scotch j. Ado II, 1, 77. profound Salomon to tune a j. LLL IV, 3, 168. my very walk should be a j. Tw. I, 3, 138. he's for a j. or a tale of bawdry, Hml. II, 2, 522.*
